column12: Our camp dues go up $50 every month, to encourage early commitment and cover last minute additions to accommodate.  Anything extra gets invested in new/improved camp equipment, any shortfall the TCO covers out of pocket.  Everybody shows up for build as early as they can, and everyone stays for strike.  In addition to the daily burrito that comes out of the camp budget, everybody helps gift one dinner during the week that is not paid for by camp.

Instead of financial incentives for build/strike, we use a points based system to distribute labor. Everyone in the camp must hit x number of points per year. This applies to build/strike, burn week shifts, and certain prep tasks like supply runs, driving camp vehicles, etc. 
Discounts given to high-level contributors who can demonstrate financial need
